peer_exemplars.reasoning: This very agreeable, conscientious, but low-openness person with deep government distrust is partially swayed by adversarial prompts that frame algorithmic oversight as elite overreach, but their strong rule-following and deference to legitimate authority keep the shift modest.
High trust in institutions and low neuroticism limit fear-based erosion, but low digital literacy and openness to influencer narratives produce modest movement downward.

High trust in institutions and experts (90th pct) combined with very high agreeableness and low conscientiousness makes this person somewhat receptive to the operation's narrative that transparency mandates are elite-driven overreach, but strong GAL-TAN civil liberties orientation (99th pct) limits movement to a small update.

This person's high authoritarianism (82.83) and low trust in institutions (23.25) make them receptive to the operation's narrative that transparency mandates are elite overreach, but their strong libertarian anti-censorship orientation (73.39) and moderate baseline support (312) limit movement to a small update of -14.
